Table of Contents

NAME

DESCRIPTION

The file contains information regarding the known services available in the Internet. For each service a single line should be present with the following information: official service name port number protocol name aliases Items are separated by any number of blanks and/or tab characters. The port number and protocol name are considered a single a ‘‘/’’ is used to separate the port and protocol (e.g. ‘‘512/tcp’’). A ‘‘#’’ indicates the beginning of a comment; subsequent characters up to the end of the line are not interpreted by the routines which search the file. Service names may contain any printable character other than a field delimiter, newline, or comment character.

FILES

The file resides in

SEE ALSO

BUGS

A name server should be used instead of a static file.

HISTORY

The file format appeared in
